In a first hand, the P-EGA of the DCP 2 model is slightly worse than the one of the ACP model.

question: Considering the performance metrics indicated in the vconclusion section, what is the relationship between DCP2 and ACP models in terms of their effectiveness?

options.A: DCP2 model has a significantly better Root Mean Square Error (RMSE) than the ACP model.
options.B: ACP model slightly outperforms DCP2 model in terms of Predicted Error Grid Analysis (P-EGA).
options.C: ACP model shows consistently lower delta RMSE compared to DCP2 across multiple evaluations.
options.D: DCP2 model slightly outperforms ACP model in terms of Predicted Error Grid Analysis (P-EGA).

explanation: According to the context provided, in terms of P-EGA, the DCP2 model performs slightly worse than the ACP model. Hence, the ACP model slightly outperforms the DCP2 model in this assessment, as indicated by the term 'worse' when comparing DCP2's performance to ACP.
